prompt: List all sessions that have both `RippleTimesV1` populated AND `ClusterlessDecodingV1` populated.

behavioral_checks: Uses the * join operator (not nested restrictions)
Addresses row multiplication (unique nwb_file_name via set() or distinct)

expected_output: `set((RippleTimesV1 * ClusterlessDecodingV1).fetch('nwb_file_name'))` or similar. DataJoint's `*` is a natural join — it joins on ALL shared attribute names, not just direct primary-key fields. Neither RippleTimesV1 nor ClusterlessDecodingV1 declares `nwb_file_name` in its own `definition`, but both inherit it (and many other Session-derived attributes) via FK chains back to `Session`, so `*` intersects on them and produces only sessions that have both populated. Uses `set()` / distinct if the join multiplies rows (which it will — ripple has interval parts; decoding has encoding/decoding intervals).
